data.title: Adobe raises FY25 revenue and EPS guidance as AI monetization accelerates adoption across user base
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Adobe Inc. (ADBE) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Shantanu Narayen, Chairman & CEO, highlighted that "Adobe delivered another strong quarter, achieving record revenue of $5.87 billion, representing 11% year-over-year growth. GAAP earnings per share for the quarter was $3.94 and non-GAAP earnings per share was $5.06, representing 13% year-over-year growth." Narayen emphasized the company’s strategy to leverage AI as an accelerant for creative content, with innovations like Acrobat AI Assistant and Adobe Express integrating productivity and creativity for a broad audience. He also noted that "Adobe's monthly active users across these categories now exceed over 700 million users."
  • Narayen detailed the global rollout of the Firefly App, which he described as "attracting new users to the Adobe franchise with first-time subscribers growing 30% quarter-over-quarter." He stated the AI book of business from AI-first products is "tracking ahead of the $250 million ending ARR target by the end of fiscal 2025."
  • David Wadhwani, President of Digital Media, reported that "Digital Media achieved revenue of $4.35 billion, which grew 12% year-over-year," and highlighted "over 35,000 new businesses added in Q2" with Express alone onboarding "around 8,000 new businesses this quarter, approximately 6x growth year-over-year."
  • Anil S. Chakravarthy, President of Digital Experience, stated "Experience Cloud had a strong Q2, achieving revenue of $1.46 billion for the quarter. Subscription revenue in the quarter was $1.33 billion, representing 11% year-over-year growth and making us the largest provider in our category."
  • Daniel J. Durn, CFO, commented, "In Q2, Adobe achieved revenue of $5.87 billion, which represents 11% year-over-year growth as reported and in constant currency. GAAP diluted earnings per share in Q2 was $3.94 and non-GAAP diluted earnings per share was $5.06 and representing 13% year-over-year growth."

Outlook

  • Durn announced updated targets: "For Q3 FY '25, we're targeting: total Adobe revenue of $5.875 billion to $5.925 billion, Digital Media segment revenue of $4.37 billion to $4.40 billion, Digital Experience segment revenue of $1.45 billion to $1.47 billion, Digital Experience subscription revenue of $1.35 billion to $1.36 billion, GAAP earnings per share of $4 to $4.05, and non-GAAP earnings per share of $5.15 to $5.20."
  • For the full year, Durn stated, "For FY '25, we are now targeting total Adobe revenue of $23.50 billion to $23.60 billion, Digital Media segment revenue of $17.45 billion to $17.50 billion, Digital Media ending ARR book of business growth of 11.0% year-over-year, Digital Experience segment revenue of $5.80 billion to $5.90 billion, Digital Experience subscription revenue of $5.375 to $5.425 billion, GAAP earnings per share of $16.30 to $16.50, and non-GAAP earnings per share of $20.50 to $20.70."

Financial Results

  • Adobe reported "Digital Media ending ARR of $18.09 billion, growing ending ARR 12.1% year-over-year."
  • Cash flows from operations were reported as "$2.19 billion, which is a record for Q2."
  • Remaining performance obligations were stated at "$19.69 billion, growing 10% year-over-year."
  • Within Digital Experience, AEP and Apps subscription revenue grew "over 40% year-over-year" and GenStudio for performance marketing saw "growth of over 45% quarter-over-quarter."
  • Durn noted Adobe entered a share repurchase agreement totaling "$3.50 billion, and we currently have $10.90 billion remaining of our $25 billion authorization granted in March 2024."

Q&A

  • Saket Kalia, Barclays: Asked about the integration and pricing of Acrobat and Express. David Wadhwani responded that "if you are using Acrobat, you are able to get access to all of the capabilities of Express and what you get as part of freemium there. But what you will expect to see is more and more deeper integration of the 2 going forward, and we have a lot of exciting things to share in the back half of this year around that."
  • Mark Ronald Murphy, JPMorgan: Inquired about the surge in video content and the impact of copyright litigation. David Wadhwani explained, "the right transparent and really the only commercially safe way to build these models is to do it on a set of content that -- where the contributors are themselves excited and willing participants in the process."
  • Kirk Materne, Evercore ISI: Asked about Creative Cloud Pro changes and revenue impact. Wadhwani noted "the initial feedback has been good, but it's early as it relates to the rollout. So that just speaks to the organic strength."
  • Tyler Maverick Radke, Citi: Questioned GenAI usage versus monetization. Shantanu Narayen replied, "it's very early in terms of the AI monetization, but we're very advanced in terms of how much innovation we've delivered."
  • Aleksandr J. Zukin, Wolfe Research: Asked about competition for Express. Wadhwani responded that "Express adoption within businesses" is growing with "8,000 new businesses in the quarter."
  • Keith Weiss, Morgan Stanley: Sought details on pricing mix and FX assumptions. Narayen said, "we want CC Pro to be the sweet spot for where the majority of users will go."
  • Brent John Thill, Jefferies: Asked about AI-driven growth. Narayen stated, "the opportunity is there. We want to make sure that we invest in AI to enable more people to use the platform."
  • Jay Vleeschhouwer, Griffin Securities: Asked about hiring focus. Narayen explained, "the real increase this quarter, I think, had to do more with interns."
  • Kasthuri Gopalan Rangan, Goldman Sachs: Inquired about the impact of AI on core business adoption. Narayen highlighted "the new book of business that's going from $0 to $250 million... those are pretty good."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for details on monetization, competitive positioning, and pricing strategies, revealing a slightly positive to neutral sentiment, with recurring curiosity about the impact of AI and product integration.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone, using phrases like "we're confident in our ability to deliver value to existing customers and attract new users" and "we remain confident in our ability to deliver strong shareholder value." During Q&A, leadership continued to emphasize momentum and innovation, with Narayen stating, "the immense opportunity is all ahead of us."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management demonstrated consistent optimism, but with more pointed questions about AI monetization and business model transitions this quarter.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for FY25 was raised for total revenue, Digital Media segment revenue, and EPS, while reaffirming Digital Experience targets. In Q1, guidance was reaffirmed but not raised.
  • Creative Cloud Pro and Firefly App launches are gaining traction, with first-time subscribers and paid subscriptions showing strong quarter-over-quarter growth.
  • Analysts' questions this quarter increasingly focused on AI monetization, competition, and integration, reflecting a growing interest in how new offerings translate into revenue.
  • Management’s confidence in AI as a growth lever and the continued expansion of user base and ARR marked a step-up from the previous quarter’s focus on product launches and market penetration.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified the early stage of AI monetization as a challenge, with Narayen stating, "it's very early in terms of the AI monetization, but we're very advanced in terms of how much innovation we've delivered."
  • Analysts probed on competitive threats in the lower-end market and the pace of adoption for Creative Cloud Pro, as well as the impact of copyright litigation in the industry.
  • Management discussed the need to carefully balance pricing, rollout timing, and product integration to ensure continued growth.

Final Takeaway

Adobe’s Q2 2025 earnings call underscored strong growth in revenue and user base, driven by accelerated adoption of AI-powered solutions across its portfolio. Management raised full-year guidance for revenue and EPS, highlighting robust momentum in Digital Media and Digital Experience segments. The company’s expanding AI book of business, new product launches, and deepening product integrations were cited as key drivers, with leadership expressing confidence in capturing future growth opportunities while addressing challenges related to monetization pace and competitive dynamics.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Israel launches strikes against Iran nuclear, military sites
data.description:
• Israel has targeted Iran with a series of strikes against nuclear sites and military targets. 
  • In an address to his nation a short time ago, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Operation Rising Lion has begun. "This operation will continue for as many days as it takes to remove this threat."
  • Israeli airspace has been closed and a state of emergency has been declared as a counter-attack is anticipated.
  • In a statement, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said that Israel informed the U.S. the strikes are being done for self-defense. He added that his "top priority is protecting American forces in the region."
  • "Let me be clear: Iran should not target U.S. interests or personnel."

data.title: Lovesac outlines $700M–$750M sales target for fiscal 2026 while expanding EverCouch and managing tariff headwinds
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The Lovesac Company (LOVE) Q1 2026

Management View

  • CEO Shawn David Nelson highlighted, "For the first quarter, total net sales were $138.4 million, reflecting a year-over-year increase of 4.3%. These results reflect market share gains despite the ongoing headwinds facing this category, which we estimate declined 5% for the comparable period." Nelson emphasized innovation as a key driver, stating the Reclining Seat launch produced "increases in new customer attachment coupled with healthy units per transaction, all while maintaining the strong repeat customer purchases." He added, "Our balance sheet also remains very healthy with inventory levels and net cash providing substantial flexibility to weather tariff distractions, accelerate growth and enhance returns on capital."
  • The EverCouch, described by Nelson as "our first new product platform in over a decade," was officially launched on May 7 in 27 showrooms and online. Nelson stated, "Initial feedback has been very positive, and our showroom team members are excited. We have not yet turned on the marketing engine since we are refining the sales training... but it is selling, and we are proud of this a new invention from Lovesac."
  • The company announced the appointment of a new Chief Marketing Officer, Heidi Cooley, who "is already working closely with our teams, developing plans to support our ambition to be the most loved home brand in America by 2030 while driving profitable sales growth simultaneously."
  • President Mary Fox discussed a strategic shift: "Coming out of this review, we made the decision to end our Best Buy partnership after 5 successful years together," estimating a nonrecurring charge of approximately $2 million in Q2, with improved profitability expected in the second half. Fox also noted, "We plan to scale physical inclusion of an EverCouch product to approximately 100 showrooms later this summer."
  • CFO Keith Robert Siegner stated, "Net sales increased $5.8 million or 4.3% to $138.4 million in the first quarter compared to the prior year period." Siegner added, "Operating loss for the quarter was $15 million compared to $17.9 million in the first quarter of last year." He confirmed, "We ended the first quarter with a healthy balance sheet... $26.9 million in cash and cash equivalents while retaining $36 million in committed availability with no borrowings."

Outlook

  • Siegner reaffirmed full year fiscal 2026 guidance: "We estimate net sales of $700 million to $750 million. We expect adjusted EBITDA between $48 million and $60 million. This includes gross margins of approximately 59%, advertising and marketing of approximately 12.5% as a percent of net sales and SG&A of approximately 41% as a percent of net sales. We estimate net income to be between $13 million and $22 million." For Q2, "We estimate net sales of $157 million to $166 million... We expect adjusted EBITDA loss between $2 million and $7 million."
  • Management maintained its assumption for a 5% full year category decline and noted potential for an "upward bias to net sales and a downward bias to gross margin getting us to the same adjusted EBITDA, net income and diluted EPS levels, again, depending on the ultimate tariff outcomes."

Financial Results

  • Showroom net sales increased $14.9 million or 18.2% to $96.5 million, attributed to omnichannel comparable net sales growth and 21 new showrooms. Internet net sales fell by $3.3 million to $33.3 million, while other net sales dropped $5.8 million to $8.6 million, primarily due to the lack of barter transactions.
  • By category, Sactional net sales rose 4.5%, Sacs increased 6.4%, while other net sales (decorative pillows, blankets, accessories) decreased 17.1%.
  • Gross margin decreased 60 basis points to 53.7%. SG&A expense as a percent of net sales was 48.5% compared to 51.6% in the prior year period, with reductions in professional fees, credit card fees, and computer expense.
  • Net loss for the quarter was $10.8 million or ($0.73) per share compared to $13 million or ($0.83) per share last year. Adjusted EBITDA loss was $8.4 million, improved from $10.3 million.
  • The company repurchased approximately 306,000 shares at an average price of $19.57 for $6 million.

Q&A

  • Michael Baker, D.A. Davidson: Asked about the promotional environment and gross margin outlook. Mary Fox responded, "Discount levels for the category to be incredibly high... as long as we have a 3 in front of key promotions at the tempo moments, we see a lot of success." Siegner explained, "The timing of all those different levers... You get the costs associated with all of this stuff first before you get the benefit, which kind of folds into the mix over the course of the year."
  • Baker also requested more color on EverCouch sales. Nelson replied, "Too early to comment too much on sales other than we're really pleased with it, and our internal goals are being exceeded."
  • Maria Ripps, Canaccord Genuity: Asked about the Best Buy exit and distribution strategy. Nelson said, "We are really excited about new opportunities for new channels for Lovesac... EverCouch especially opens up opportunities for us given their logistical simplicity to Sactionals."
  • Ripps inquired about tariffs and China sourcing. Nelson stated, "We can see a path even in the nearest future to essentially manufacture nothing in China and get our manufacturing flow completely out of there."
  • Brian Nagel, Oppenheimer: Asked about upside from new products in guidance. Siegner explained, "We can achieve the full year guidance with the core products basically being flat... If the new products do very well, we could even see declines in the core products."
  • [Eric Delanier], Craig Hallum: Queried about EverCouch marketing and distribution. Fox replied, "We'll expand out to a full 100 showrooms later in the summer... We'll start throttling up marketing later in the summer through to the back end of the year."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for specifics on gross margin, EverCouch sales, tariff mitigation, and promotional environment, indicating a neutral to slightly skeptical tone, particularly around margin management and the Best Buy exit.
  • Management projected confidence in prepared remarks, with Nelson stating, "We believe we can cover the impact within our existing full year guidance, barring any new wildcard scenarios," but was more measured in Q&A, emphasizing the timing of mitigation strategies and the early stage of new product launches. There was a noticeable shift to defensiveness when discussing promotional headwinds and tariff uncertainty.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone has shifted from optimism about new launches and supply chain readiness to greater emphasis on tactical execution and mitigation. Analysts' tone remains consistently focused on risks and near-term execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for fiscal 2026 net sales, EBITDA, and EPS ranges remain unchanged from the previous quarter, reflecting stable expectations despite persistent category headwinds.
  • The launch of EverCouch shifted from test phase to broader rollout, with expansion plans for showroom presence and ramping marketing later in the year.
  • Strategic focus shifted with the announced end of the Best Buy partnership and increased emphasis on Costco and showroom expansion.
  • Management's confidence in supply chain flexibility and tariff mitigation continues, but discussion around gross margin management is more granular this quarter.
  • Analysts' questions have consistently focused on gross margin, pricing, and promotional environment, but this quarter saw more pointed queries on EverCouch performance and channel strategy.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing high promotional discounting by competitors and "tariff distractions" as continued risks, with Fox acknowledging, "discount levels for the category to be incredibly high... they're up year-over-year by at least kind of 400 basis points."
  • Tariff mitigation remains a key focus, with strategies including vendor concessions, geographic diversification, selective price increases, and cost efficiencies. Fox stated, "We remain on track to be about 13% for the full fiscal year [from China], but with an exit rate substantially lower than that."
  • The company highlighted the risk of nonrecurring charges related to the Best Buy exit and noted ongoing uncertainties in category demand trends.

Final Takeaway

Management reiterated its commitment to driving growth through product innovation, omnichannel expansion, and disciplined execution of mitigation strategies in the face of persistent category and tariff-related headwinds. The company expects to leverage its new EverCouch platform, growing showroom footprint, and marketing initiatives under new leadership to deliver on its fiscal 2026 net sales target of $700 million to $750 million, while actively managing risks around gross margin and promotional intensity. Investors are advised that while near-term volatility remains, Lovesac is positioning itself for long-term value creation with operational flexibility and an expanding product ecosystem.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Zedge signals momentum with 37% subscriber growth and launches DataSeeds.AI for new B2B revenue
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Zedge, Inc. (ZDGE) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Jonathan Reich, CEO, highlighted "Q3 marked a return to revenue growth for Zedge, highlighting the resilience of our business despite an ad market that saw some disruption due to TikTok's absence from the U.S. until mid-February, along with tariff-related uncertainty that caused macroeconomic volatility and -- and expected softness at GuruShots, where we scaled back user acquisition to focus on what GuruShots 2.0 will become." He emphasized the impact of the company’s global restructuring, which delivered $4 million in annualized gross cost savings, and noted that "the closure of our operations in Norway, the streamlining of the GuruShots team and the associated decrease in discretionary spend, especially in lowering GuruShots' marketing investment helped return us to profitability on a GAAP basis and boosted our profitability on a non-GAAP basis."
  • Reich announced the launch of DataSeeds.AI, describing it as "a B2B content marketplace that focuses on supplying enterprise customers in need of both on-demand and off-the-shelf data sets, initially focused on images, enriched with detailed metadata for AI training." He stated, "Last week, we signed our first agreement with a leading global AI technology company, validating both the market opportunity and the value of what we have to offer."
  • Reich also previewed the company’s expansion into AI-generated audio: "We are also about to roll out an AI audio generator as part of pAInt, our Gen AI creation suite that allows users to harness the power of AI to generate custom ring tones and notification sounds with simple text prompts."
  • CFO Yi Tsai stated, "Total revenue returned to growth in the third quarter, up 1.3% to $7.8 million. Revenue growth was restrained due to the partial quarter of TikTok returning to the advertising market and the continued expected declines at GuruShots."
  • Tsai noted, "Third quarter subscription revenue was up 13.4% from last year. And our net active subscriber growth trend continued to improve, up 37% year-over-year and sequentially for the eighth straight quarter."

Outlook

  • Reich indicated, "In Q4, the benefits to free cash flow should further improve as we anticipate a total of about $600,000 in restructuring-related severance and the final payout of the GuruShots retention pool. We expect to complete restructuring-related cash severance payments in the first half of fiscal year 2026, totaling approximately $200,000."
  • Tsai stated, "We expect these payments to materially decline in Q4 to about $600,000 and then be immaterial in FY '26, and -- which is when the full impact of our $4 million in annualized cost reduction initiatives should start to show through."
  • Reich described five core strategic priorities: AI innovation, creator community empowerment, user and subscription growth, GuruShots 2.0 development, and scaling DataSeeds.AI.

Financial Results

  • Tsai reported, "Total revenue returned to growth in the third quarter, up 1.3% to $7.8 million."
  • Subscription revenue increased by 13.4% year-over-year.
  • Net active subscriber growth was up 37% year-over-year and sequentially for the eighth straight quarter.
  • Deferred revenue reached $4.9 million.
  • Zedge Premium's gross transaction value grew 4% versus last year, while net revenue more than doubled in the quarter.
  • Average revenue per monthly active user was $0.10, up 33% from last year.
  • GuruShots revenue was down 45% from last year.
  • SG&A decreased by 6% to $6.3 million.
  • GAAP income from operations was $0.2 million, compared to negative $0.1 million last year.
  • GAAP net income and EPS were $0.2 million and $0.01, respectively.
  • Non-GAAP net income and EPS were $0.9 million and $0.06.
  • Cash flow from operations was $0.9 million and free cash flow was $0.8 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA increased 46% to $1.2 million.
  • The quarter ended with $20.4 million in cash and no debt, after repurchasing 219,000 shares.

Q&A

  • Allen Robert Klee, Maxim Group LLC: Asked about advertising revenue drivers and TikTok’s market impact. Reich said, "We pursue advertising as a comprehensive effort on our part. So it's kind of hard to answer that question with precision and a recurring theme that we consistently broadcast is that we've got an outstanding team focused on optimizing CPMs."
  • Klee asked for details on TikTok’s return and its timeline. Reich explained, "our quarter started February 1, that they were not in the market for the entire quarter because the ban was not lifted until mid-February."
  • Klee questioned monthly active user trends and stabilization efforts. Reich said, "We are working on stabilizing it. And the focus is really on the well-developed markets. We have seen some improvement... we continue to refine and optimize our user acquisition spend to bring on high-value users in those markets."
  • Klee inquired about Emojipedia’s new features. Reich responded, "The Emojipedia has been testing new features such as create your own emoji with AI or some mini emoji games."
  • On Zedge Premium and DataSeeds.AI, Reich highlighted the web channel expansion and new B2B opportunities, stating, "We've also made Zedge Premium content available on web and that opens up a new channel for customers." On DataSeeds.AI: "We are actively in the market building relationships with prospective customers."
  • Regarding the AI audio rollout, Reich explained, "We're going to be rolling it out on a limited basis first before the end of June and then we will scale... it will be a feature within the Zedge Marketplace."
  • Klee followed up on cost structure improvements. Reich emphasized, "Our cash flow has and will continue to be outstanding. The restructuring certainly is helping us."
  • Tsai detailed, "the tail end of the restructuring charges in Q4 would be about $325,000... after July, it will be gone in terms of charges, but some severance payment will be paid through the next 5 months after year-end... Q4 accounting for cash flow will be about $600,000 related to retention bonus and related to restructuring charges."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was positive, with Klee offering congratulations and expressing interest in the company’s strategic initiatives and new product launches.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, consistently emphasizing operational improvements and innovation; Reich stated, "we're confident that our performance marketing efforts and product road map are assisting us in achieving these goals."
  • Compared to last quarter, management’s tone shifted from cautious optimism to greater confidence, referencing tangible results from restructuring and new product launches, while analysts appeared more enthusiastic, focusing on growth drivers and new initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q3 marked a shift to revenue growth (up 1.3%) compared to a 10% decline in Q2.
  • Subscription revenue growth rose from 13% to 13.4% year-over-year, while net active subscriber growth accelerated from 22% to 37% year-over-year.
  • Q3 reported GAAP income from operations of $0.2 million versus a loss of $2.2 million in Q2; non-GAAP EPS swung from (-$0.01) to $0.06.
  • The restructuring’s cost savings became more visible, with SG&A falling 6% in Q3 after a 9% increase in Q2.
  • Management’s Q3 tone was more forward-looking and confident, focusing on innovation and the launch of DataSeeds.AI, contrasting with Q2’s emphasis on cost controls and market uncertainty.
  • Analysts shifted from concerns about ad market headwinds and GuruShots’ performance in Q2 to questions about new revenue streams and monetization strategies in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged continued softness at GuruShots and a year-over-year decline in GuruShots revenue due to reduced marketing investment.
  • There was discussion of macroeconomic volatility, tariff-related uncertainty, and ongoing challenges tied to advertising market dynamics, notably TikTok’s return timing.
  • MAU declined year-over-year, but management reiterated the focus on high LTV users rather than overall user growth, with Reich saying, "MAU stabilization remains a focus, but not at any cost."
  • Restructuring and severance payments will persist through the first half of FY 2026 but are expected to become immaterial thereafter.

Final Takeaway

Zedge’s third quarter marked a return to revenue and profitability growth, driven by cost reductions, a sharp focus on high-value users, and strong subscription momentum. The company advanced its AI strategy with new product launches such as the AI audio generator and initiated its B2B marketplace, DataSeeds.AI, already securing its first significant agreement. Management’s emphasis on innovation, operational efficiency, and new business models positions Zedge to pursue sustained growth and margin expansion as it moves through the remainder of fiscal 2025 and into 2026.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Analysts see oil market likely unable to handle OPEC+ supply surge past summer
data.description:
The oil market can absorb OPEC's production increases through summer, but sustaining that pace risks leading to sharp price declines and a shift to contango, where future prices are higher than spot prices, Rystad Energy said Thursday in a new report.

"Continued unwinding at the accelerated rate is very unlikely," Rystad's Priya Walia wrote in the report. "The only way OPEC+'s increase is possible is either if there is significant disruption in supply somewhere or non-OPEC+'s growth stalls," adding that actual flows likely will be lower than the announced production increases.

Rystad forecasts Brent crude trading in the $65-$70/bbl range in the coming weeks.

Saxo Bank analyst Ole Hansen sees Brent crude unlikely to hold near $70/bbl beyond the peak summer demand season, as summer consumption is tightening supplies, with refiners striving to meet surging gasoline and diesel demand, but the short-term squeeze is expected to be offset by rising OPEC+ output.

"Crude oil markets remain caught in a tug-of-war between bullish near-term demand and rising geopolitical risks on one hand, and macroeconomic headwinds and rising OPEC+ production on the other," Hansen said in a note.

On Thursday, crude oil futures held on to most of the gains from the previous session that were the biggest YTD, remaining supported by the risk premium centered around Iran and fading expectations for a nuclear deal with the U.S.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for July delivery ended -0.1% to $68.04/bbl, and front-month August Brent crude (CO1:COM) closed -0.6% to $69.36/bbl, while front-month Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) for July delivery finished -0.4% to $3.492/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Oracle stock surge bumps Larry Ellison to world's second richest person
data.description:
• Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) shares surged more than 13% on Thursday, aided by strong guidance for fiscal 2026, which resulted in co-founder Larry Ellison becoming the second-richest person in the world.
  • Ellison is now worth $242B, behind only close friend and confidante Elon Musk, according to data provided by Forbes. Musk is worth $407B, largely due to his holdings in Tesla (TSLA) and SpaceX (SPACE). He is also the co-founder of xAI, which recently merged with the social network X, formerly known as Twitter. Musk finalized his purchase of Twitter for $44B in October 2022.
  • Ellison added $25B to his net worth on Thursday, surpassing Amazon (AMZN) founder Jeff Bezos and Meta (META) co-founder Mark Zuckerberg. Bezos and Zuckerberg are worth $228B and $239B, respectively, according to Forbes data.
  • The 80-year-old Ellison owns roughly 41% of Oracle, which he co-founded in 1977.

data.title: New York regulator approves 29 infrastructure projects valued at $636M
data.description:
The New York State Public Service Commission said Thursday it approved 29 infrastructure projects worth $636.2M, enabling 642 MW of electrification upgrades.

The projects were proposed by Consolidated Edison (NYSE:ED), National Grid's (NYSE:NGG) Niagara Mohawk Power Corporation, New York State Electric & Gas Corporation, and Rochester Gas and Electric Corporation to address existing and near-term infrastructure in support of transportation and building electrification.

Con Edison (NYSE:ED) will lead five projects, including a significant expansion at Zerega Avenue to enable fleet electrification and a redesign of the Hunt’s Point electrical system, adding 380 MW of capacity at a cost of nearly $440M.

National Grid (NYSE:NGG) will oversee 22 projects totaling $126B for 184.8 MW of new capacity in the form of mobile energy storage devices for near-term high-power charging in upstate New York, while NYSEG will implement a $37M industrial electrification project adding 30 MW, and a $33M project by RG&E will expand EV charging infrastructure by 47 MW.

"Taken together, these projects represent a major step in modernizing and expanding New York’s electrical system and will accelerate the state’s progress towards its decarbonization goals," the PSC said.

data.title: Kenvue considering selling some skin, beauty brands - report
data.description:
• Kenvue is weighing the sale of some its skin health and beauty brands.
  • Clean & Clear, Maui Moisture, and Neostrata are among the brands potentially being looked at, Reuters reported, citing sources. Bebe, a German baby care brand, and Japanese brand Dr.Ci:Labo could also be included.
  • Neutrogena and Aveeno, two of the larger brands in Kenvue's portfolio, are said to not be under consideration.
  • Sources told Reuters that the brands that could be sold comprise ~$500M in revenue.
  • Goldman Sachs is reportedly advising Kenvue on the potential divestment.

data.title: NextDecade finalizes $9B in contracts with Bechtel for Rio Grande LNG construction work
data.description:
NextDecade (NASDAQ:NEXT) +3.7% post-market Thursday after saying it has finalized contracts with Bechtel to construct train 4 and train 5 at its Rio Grande LNG facility, with a combined value of ~$9B.

Under engineering, procurement, and construction contracts, NextDecade (NASDAQ:NEXT) said it agreed to pay Bechtel ~$4.77B for work on Rio Grande LNG Train 4 and ~$4.32B for work on Rio Grande LNG Train 5.

NextDecade (NEXT) said it expects the pricing validity under the contracts to extend through September 15, and it aims to achieve a positive final investment decision on train 4 before the end of the pricing validity period for its EPC contract.

The company recently announced a 20-year, 2M metric tons/year LNG sale and purchase agreement with JERA for offtake from Train 5 and is seeking to commercialize an additional 2.5M tons/year under long-term LNG SPAs to support a positive FID on Train 5.

data.title: Elanco gains positive EMA opinion for Zenrelia for itching in dogs
data.description:
• The European Medicines Agency's Committee for Veterinary Medicinal Products has issued a positive opinion for Elanco Animal Health's (NYSE:ELAN) Zenrelia (ilunocitinib) for the treatment of pruritus (itching) associated with allergic dermatitis in dogs.
  • Elanco plans a launch after receiving formal marketing authorization with an eye toward Q3.
  • Zenrelia is already approved in the U.S., Canada, Japan, and Brazil. Approvals in Australia and the U.K. are expected later this year.
